LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT LORD EBALO'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS DENIED BECAUSE IT WAS FILED MORE THAN SIX MONTHS AFTER ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND THERE IS NO REASON WHY VACATING THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT ALONE WOULD NOT BE AN IDLE ACT. (SEE RUTAN VS. SUMMIT SPORTS, INC., 1985 173 CAL. APP. 3RD 965). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Apr-10-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LORD EBALO'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is denied because it was filed more than six months after entry of default and there is no reason why vacating the default judgment alone would not be an idle act. (See Rutan vs. Summit Sports, Inc., 1985 173 Cal. App. 3rd 965). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (301/PJB/CK) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
